rulings.Case Title: NANCY J YEE VS. EDWARD YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Edward Yee And Jenny Granados' Motion For Sanctions, Attorneys Fees And Costs Against Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 27, 2013, Line 23, Defendants Edward Yee And Jenny Granados' Motion For Sanctions, Attorneys Fees And Costs Against Plaintiff. Granted in part. The Court orders Plaintiff to pay Defendants $956 which the Court finds are expenses actually incurred solely because of the cancellation. This amount includes $155 for Defendant Granados's non-refundable airfare, $166 change fee for Defendants' counsel's flight, $135 for the filing fee and CourtCall service fee and $500 in attorney fees for making this motion. The Court finds that additional sanctions are not warranted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4 pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KIM TIMMONS VS. TRADER JOE'S COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Requests For Production Of Documents; Requests For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Her Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 27, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT TRADER JOE'S COMPANY, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Requests For Production Of Documents; Requests For Monetary Sanctions Against Pltf And Her Counsel Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Both motions granted, no sanction as no opposition filed by responding party as required by CCP §2031.300/2030.290 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Continuance
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 27, 2013, Line 5, ASSIGNEE WILLIAM BUCHANAN's Motion For Continuance. The Court treats the motion for continuance as a motion for relief per CCP 473. The evidence demonstrates that plaintiff never received the motion to vacate that was heard and granted as unopposed on February 27, 2013. The Court vacates the February 27, 2013 order. Defendant shall re-file and re-serve the motion to vacate. rulings.Case Title: EDDIE VALADEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Supplemental Pltf'S Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 27, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFF EDDIE VALADEZ' Motion To Supplemental Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint. The motion is denied. Plaintiff is attempting to plead new theories of liability that are unrelated to the chain of events that were originally pleaded. A party cannot allege an entirely new cause of action in a supplemental complaint. See Flood v. Simpson, (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 644, 647. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BYRA MARBLE VS. ORGANON USA, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Mckesson Corporation'S Joinder In Merck Defendants' Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 27, 2013, Line 20, Defendant Mckesson Corporation'S Joinder In Merck Defendants' Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens. The Court denies the motion as to Ms. Byra Marble. She is a California resident and Defendant fails to show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum with regard to her claim. The Court notes that Defendant McKesson is also a California resident. The Court grants the motion and stays the action as to the non-California residents and severs their claims. The torts did not occur in California, the Court can reasonably assume that the sources of proof and physicians remain in the home states based on the Fact Sheets filed in federal court, and California has little interest in adjudicating their claims. The presence of McKesson is less compelling as to these plaintiffs. See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc., (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 756. The granting of the motion is conditioned on defendants agreeing to submit to jurisdiction in the home states of plaintiffs, and defendants continuing to abide by their statement in their moving papers that they agree that the statute of limitations is tolled as of the filing of this lawsuit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, $340 sanction per CCP §2023.030(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: DERRICK RICHARDS WHITE VS. U.C.S.F. MEDICAL CENTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 27, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with 45 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs have leave to seek appointment as guardian ad litem and plead a claim for medical malpractice, breach of contract, and/or fraud or concealment on behalf of their daughter, if they can allege facts in good faith to support those claims and as set forth below. The demurrer to the intentional tort cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead fraud or concealment with more specificity. Plaintiff fails to cite facts showing fraud or concealment. The demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is sustained. Plaintiff fails to allege an express contract promising a particular result. See Depenbrok v. Kaiser (1978) 79 Cal.App.3d 167, 170-171. Further, Plaintiffs do not have standing to assert this breach of contract claim as parents; it must be asserted as guardian ad litem of the child. The Court rejects Defendant's res judicata/collateral estoppel argument. The federal court dismissed the federal action based on Eleventh Amendment immunity and Defendant fails to show that the ruling was on the merits or that Defendant's alleged malpractice was actually litigated. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO AMADO VAZ VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 27, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC., RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY, CLIFFORD GILMORE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Hearing required. The Court is inclined to rule as follows: Overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer to the first and second causes of action for failure to exhaust administrative remedies is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts showing that he has obtained a right to sue letter from the Department of Fair Housing and Employment for these claims. The demurrer to the first, second, and third causes of action as to the individual defendants Shaw, Tekkey, and Gilmore is sustained without leave to amend. Only employers are liable for these causes of action. The demurrer to the first cause of action for failure to state a claim is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to allege sufficient facts for a claim of race or national origin discrimination. Plaintiff is given leave to plead further facts that give rise to his claim that he was treated differently on account of race or national origin, and facts concerning acts of discrimination or retaliation on account of his lack of citizenship. The demurrer to the third cause of action for failure to state a claim is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim for wrongful discharge based on disability discrimination or retaliation. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. All of the conduct giving rise to the claim stems from the employment relationship and falls within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Worker's Compensation Appeals Board. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting unfair business practices. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting a present controversy. If Plaintiff files an amended complaint, it must be filed within 20 days after entry of this Order. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO AMADO VAZ VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 27, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC., RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY, CLIFFORD GILMORE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Hearing required. The Court is inclined to rule as follows: Granted in part and denied in part. The motion to strike all references to malice, oppression, and fraud is denied. These claims are not subject to a heightened pleading standard. The motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is denied. The claim is sufficiently pled. The motion to strike all prayers for attorney's fees is granted without leave to amend. The Plaintiff is pro per and not a licensed attorney. The motion to strike the portions of the prayers that are not a demand for judgment is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 425.10 does not require otherwise. The motion to strike paragraph (c) is granted as to the statement "damages for unpaid wages, including regular and overtime wages" with leave to amend. The complaint has alleged no facts indicating unpaid wages. The motion to strike paragraph (f) of the prayer is granted with leave to amend. The complaint has alleged no facts indicating that plaintiff was denied access to any records. The motion to strike paragraph (g) of the prayer is granted with leave to amend in light of the Court's ruling on the related cause of action for declaratory relief. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting a present controversy. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed. =(302/MJM)
